data.content: Starting the notification service refactor. I'll look at the current architecture...

data.summary: Refactoring the notification service from a monolithic mailer into an event-driven architecture. Current state: have mapped all 12 notification types, identified 4 that can be consolidated. Using SQS for async processing. Redis pub/sub for real-time notifications. The webhook endpoint is at /api/webhooks/notify. Primary concern: ensuring zero message loss during migration.

data.content: The SQS queues are set up. Now let's add the dead letter queue config.
Configured DLQ with maxReceiveCount of 3 and 14-day retention...

data.summary:
## Main Thread
Refactoring notification service to event-driven architecture

## Active Sub-threads (max 3)
1. SQS queue setup — Queues created, DLQ configured with maxReceiveCount=3
2. Real-time notifications — Redis pub/sub channel design

## Key State
- webhookEndpoint: /api/webhooks/notify
- sqsQueueUrl: https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456/notifications
- dlqRetention: 14 days
- maxReceiveCount: 3
- notificationTypes: 12 total, 4 consolidating

## Unresolved
- Zero-downtime migration plan not finalized
- Redis connection pooling config needed

## Summary
Migrated from monolithic mailer to event-driven. SQS queues operational with dead letter queue safety net. Next phase is Redis pub/sub for real-time and the zero-downtime cutover plan.

threadSnapshot.mainThread: Refactoring notification service to event-driven architecture

subThreads.name: SQS queue setup
subThreads.detail: Queues created, DLQ configured with maxReceiveCount=3
